John, a junior software engineer. He studied computer science for his undergraduate degree at the University of Bristol and recently graduated. He did an internship in a small startup at the end of his third year where he assisted senior developers to build a tool that helped companies to track and manage their inventory. He has experience with Python, AWS, and unit testing.

Brad, a principal software engineer at Amazon. He has 12 years of professional software development experience and has led large-scale projects in industry. Brad has knowledge of object-oriented design, data structures, and algorithms. He also has experience designing and building large-scale systems in a multi-tiered, distributed environment. In his current role at the Amazon Device Management Systems organisation, he works on device-authentication, device-registration, device profiles and account-linking and is now working to expand to Remote Key provisioning and Passkeys.

Harold, an English teacher who has never used a programming language. He understands how to use Excel and basic word processing on a computer but has no knowledge of data structures, programming, or computing concepts.
